title: Adachi and the Zebra
description: Adachi comes home to find a zebra in her apartment—Yashiro, wearing a zebra costume. A short, whimsical glimpse into their strange bond.
content:
I was usually the one who came home late, but today, for once, I returned earlier than Shimamura. Even though she'd told me beforehand and I was prepared for it, stepping into a dark, empty apartment still sent a chill crawling up my legs—a cold completely out of season. As if I had stepped on the corner of a sheet of black origami and creased it the wrong way. Unsettling, somehow.

Back when I used to come home to an empty room all the time, it never bothered me.

But that was because I had never known anything else. Because I had never felt anything warmer. In a way… maybe that numbness had been a kind of strength.

Now, I'd become weak. But even in that weakness, I was still living—and that made me happy.

I turned on the lights, dropped my bag, and collapsed onto the sofa. As I slowly sank into it, I let out a long breath. It felt like the ropes tied around my shoulders were being loosened one by one. And when all the air left my lungs, my whole body grew so heavy I felt like I might drown in the cushions.

Staring blankly at the ceiling, I thought:

Shimamura is still at work. Right, work. Even though it's just work, the fact she's coming home late makes me uneasy.

Where does that unease come from?

Probably because she's popular at her company. Everyone likes talking to her. People gravitate to her. Or something like that.

We work at different companies, and I've never seen what she's like at her workplace. She talks about it sometimes, but I can't picture it clearly. And so…

How should I put it…? I get a little defensive.

If the entire world were made up of copies of me, no version of me would take my eyes off Shimamura for more than half an hour. I know that's impossible, but still—in my heart, that's how precious she is.

So anyway…

I worry. A lot.

Because to me, Shimamura only has beautiful sides. And I can't help imagining others seeing her that way too. I hold my forehead and wrestle with my own jealousy, 'Ugghhhhhh…'

I was still in my work clothes. What was I even doing? Don't I have anything better to do?

'…Do I? …Do I really…?'

Besides waiting for Shimamura to come home, was there anything else worth doing?

I felt like a dog—or a cat—waiting for its owner to return.

'No, no… at the very least I should make dinner.'

And tidy up the apartment and do laundry while I had the time. I should save all of that for when she's not around. Time spent with Shimamura—every second needs to be treasured.

I headed into the bedroom to change. Whenever I opened the closet, my eyes wandered toward that brightly colored China dress. There haven't been any reasons where I needed to wear it, but sometimes Shimamura asks me to…Which, now that I think about it, makes it sound like some questionable kind of game.

After folding my clothes away, I was about to check the fridge when I heard water running from the sink. I turned—And there, standing on tiptoe at the faucet, holding a cup under the water, was…

A zebra.

'……………………………………………………'

To be precise, a girl wearing a zebra costume—or maybe a zebra-patterned pajama suit.

She filled her cup, then gulped it down—glug glug glug sip sip sip—while turning her head toward me.

Her eyes were water-blue, too vivid to belong to any living creature. Looking into them was like staring into a star-filled night sky. Were those even human eyes?

'Good eveniiing~'

She greeted cheerfully, still drinking—how does she even do that?

'…G-good evening.'

I managed to reply. But the real question was—why was she here?

'You're here again…'

'I'm here again~,' she said proudly as she pattered over to me.

I stiffened completely. I wasn't good with kids—and this one was especially strange.

'I promised Shimamura I wouldn't go to her workplace.'

'…And?'

'So I came to see Adachi instead~.'

She laughed cheerfully as if that explained everything. I wasn't convinced the logic held up.

The door had been shut tight—where had she even come from?

She giggled smugly and held up her cup. On it, written in Shimamura's handwriting, was a single kanji: 「社」 (Yashiro).

'Shimamura bought this cup just for me!'

'Mm…'

Shimamura really spoils this one. She does pamper me too, but still… I can't help feeling a little annoyed.

So her name was Yashiro…

Until now, I knew nothing except her name and her appearance. It felt like she hadn't grown at all since our school days.

What was she to Shimamura, anyway?

'What… are you, exactly?'

All my questions condensed into that one line.

Yashiro answered: 'I exist everywhere! Anywhere can be me, and I can be anything. In any form. I can change freely into whatever I want. So I'm in every corner of the world—and of course, here too!'

She spoke softly like a child, but her words were oddly philosophical.

A talking zebra honestly sounded like something out of a fairy tale.

'I'm with Shimamura, and I come to see Adachi too. I'm with Shou, with Mama and Papa, with Hino and Nagafuji, and one day, I'll be with Chito too.'

Some names I recognized, some I'd never heard.

'That's who I am~.'

'…I didn't understand a word of that.'

'Is that so~?'

She set her empty cup down with a thunk.

'Well then, don't worry about it~.'

'…O-okay.'

She was definitely hard to deal with.

But she also wasn't influenced by anything we did—she simply existed at her own pace. In a way, that was… strangely calming.

…Or maybe not.

'So I'll be with you now~!'

And so the mysterious zebra ran around after me through the apartment, calling 'Waa~ waa~' as it went.

A Little Later

'I'm home—oh.'

I followed the lit room and peeked inside—only to see a zebra head sticking out from the sofa.

'Oh?'

Circling around to the front, I saw the scene clearly, 'When did you two get so close?'

Adachi was sprawled on the sofa, sleeping with the zebra pressed against her. Listening to their synchronized breathing, I couldn't help but smile.
